messages.content: isn't it wild how a coffee that was like 2 bucks when i was a teenager is basically 6 now

Inflation hits differently when you can map it to something you bought a hundred times. Coffee's a good yardstick — familiar enough that the number actually lands.

Is there something underneath that, or just a moment of sticker shock?
i was telling my coworker, feels like everything just quietly crept up and we all kinda accepted it

That quiet acceptance is worth noticing — it happens gradually enough that there's no single moment to push back against.

What made you bring it up with your coworker today?
makes me a little nostalgic for the days when a 20 in your pocket felt like real money lol

That's a specific kind of loss — not dramatic, just a slow erosion of what a thing used to mean.

What does that nostalgia point to for you — the money itself, or something about that time?
anyway not complaining really, just one of those shower thoughts about how prices change over time
Makes sense — sometimes a thought just wants to be noticed, not solved.
